_doc: Editor steering notes for the conference preview agent. Authoritative — these override prior knowledge and any conflicting context-data interpretation. Keep each note to 1–3 short sentences. Empty strings are dropped silently. Loaded by build_conference_context.py from /cfb-research/writer_notes/<season>/<conf>.json.
_usage: conference_note: 1–3 sentences with two purposes — (a) the meta-narrative for the year (e.g., 'The realignment dust has settled but the talent gap between the top three and the rest is still widening.'), and (b) calibration the agent needs to right-size confidence: what 'good' looks like in this league. SEC example: 'In modern SEC: 7-1 in conf typically reaches the title game; 9-0 has never happened. 9 overall wins is a historic season.' MAC example: 'In the MAC, 8 conference wins is championship caliber; 6-2 contends.' Combine narrative + calibration when both matter.

team_notes.<slug>: 1–3 sentences about that specific team — what the data won't tell the agent (a coaching change, a critical transfer, an injury, an off-field story, the angle the writeup should take).
Slugs match the canonical team slugs in build_team_context.py / scrape_team_context.py.
conference_note: Three storylines define the 2026 SEC: (1) the league who's motto is 'It Just Means More' hasn't played for a national title since the 2022 season and the Big Ten has caught it at the top; (2) the SEC remains the deepest conference in football; (3) the move to a nine-game conference schedule is itself a top story — does the extra in-league game hurt CFP résumés (more losses) or help them (tougher games played)? Conference-record calibration for 2026 (apply these bands when framing projected or actual records — they override generic win-total framing): 9-0 in conf would be historic; 8-1 is the realistic ceiling and makes the SEC title game; 7-2 also makes the title game; 6-3 contends; 5-4 is a good SEC season for most teams. A 'tremendous season' here is 8-1 in conf, not nine overall wins. SEC tier framework (used in team_notes; tiers describe team caliber, NOT exact record — record varies with schedule strength): Tier 1 = SEC title-game favorite if healthy and CFP-bound; Tier 2 = title-game contender, CFP contender; Tier 3 = middle tier and title-game outsider, in the contention mix but outside CFP; Tier 4 = rebuilding year, fighting to get to a bowl game; Tier 5 = down year, where reaching bowl eligibility would itself be the ceiling. When team_notes give a range (e.g., 'Tier 2-3'), the agent should treat the wider boundary as the realistic downside and pick a centering tier in the writeup based on health and early-season tape.

team_notes.alabama: QB1 Keelon Russell vs Austin Mack unresolved — treat as unsettled across all sections of the writeup. HC DeBoer's new contract helps ease tensions. Offense, particularly run game and Offensive Line must improve. Tier 2-3 SEC team.
team_notes.arkansas: New HC Silverfield, hired from G6 school Memphis, hasn't won over fans yet, QB1 KJ Jackson vs AJ Hill key storyline — treat as unsettled across all sections of the writeup. A bowl bid would be a great season for Arkansas. Defense has been bottom SEC. Tier 4-5 SEC team.
team_notes.auburn: Fans starving for new HC Alex Golesh to fix the offense and program; DC Durkin back is key but strange. QB Byrum Brown from South Florida (along with 12 others with Golesh & staff) key storyline. Likely not a contender, but could surprise some teams. Have not won at Alabama since 2010, on current 0-6 streak overall.  Tier 3-4 SEC team.
team_notes.florida: New HC Jon Sumrall popular but needs to fix the offense, QB1 Aaron Philo vs Tramell Jones Jr. key storyline — treat as unsettled across all sections of the writeup. New OC Buster Faulkner a great hire from Georgia Tech, brought QB Aaron Philo with him. Likely not a contender, but could surprise some teams. Have beaten rival Florida State two years in a row. Have real weapons with RB Jadan Baugh and at WR with Vernell Brown III and Dallas Wilson who flashed as freshman but must remain healthy. Tier 2-4 SEC team.
team_notes.georgia: SEC back-to-back champs, but 0-2 in CFP quarterfinals in the last two years is disappointing (have had a bye in round 1 both years). Defense must improve consistency. WR is a big question but Tight End room is loaded with size and talent. Tier 1 SEC team, one of the favorites to win the SEC this year but not a lock. HC Kirby Smart won back to back titles in 2021 and 2022, NIL era has been different though.
team_notes.kentucky: Renewed hope with new HC Stein, especially on offense after the Stoops era went stale. Tier 4-5 SEC team.
team_notes.lsu: Kiffin a proven Offensive mastermind, returns to play vs Ole Miss in Week 3. Blake Baker retained as DC, big for defense; QB Leavitt recovery from foot injury last October (while at Arizona State) key. Kiffin returns to Oxford in week 3 when LSU plays at Ole Miss, a highly anticipated game. Tier 1-3 SEC team.
team_notes.mississippi-state: Prev HC Zach Arnett back as DC; QB Taylor key to fans hope of escaping the SEC cellar. Last Egg Bowl win was 2022, 1-5 stretch vs Ole Miss. Tier 4-5 SEC team.
team_notes.missouri: QB Austin Simmons (transferred IN from Ole Miss — new arrival, NOT returning) is the big storyline; defense is a big rebuild job under popular DC Corey Batoon. New OC Chip Lindsey has a lot to prove as his offenses at Michigan in 2025 and North Carolina in 2023-2024 were not good.  Tier 2-3 SEC team.
team_notes.oklahoma: Offense has been the big problem, run game especially. Season will be determined by whether the offensive line has been fixed, as it has been a constant issue for years now. QB1 Mateer wasn't the same last year after a thumb injury, from which he has recovered. Added WRs Trell Harris and Parker Livingston via transfer portal to pair with electric Isaiah Sategna to finally give OU more weapons. Defense has been dominant. Lost two straight and on 1-3 streak vs Texas in Red River Rivalry. Tier 2-3 SEC team.
team_notes.ole-miss: Coming off best season maybe ever; want HC Golding to prove he's better than Lane Kiffin (left for LSU last year). Ole Miss get a chance at revenge on Kiffin when they host LSU in week 3, one of CFBs most anticipated matchups. QB Trinidad Chambliss won his case for another year of eligibility, a big boost to the offense and team after his magical performance in the CFP in January.  Tier 2-3 SEC team.
team_notes.south-carolina: HC Beamer underappreciated but last year was bad. Rebuilt OL, but injuries already a problem at OT, hampers trying to fix this offense. Tier 3-4 SEC team.
team_notes.tennessee: MacIntyre projected to open as starter, but 5★ Faizon Brandon competing for the job — treat as unsettled across all sections of the writeup. New DC Jim Knowles hired away from Penn State to fix a poor Tenn defense last year, although they've added good talent.  Portal DE Chaz Coleman missed spring for mental health and may not play this year. Did not beat team w winning record LY. Tier 2-3 SEC team.
team_notes.texas: Defining year for HC Sark and the program; they've been building to make a deep CFP run in '26 with QB Manning & portal signings. OL the big question on team. Texas is 0-3 vs Georgia since joining the SEC. Tier 1-2 SEC team, one of the favorites to win the conference.
team_notes.texas-am: Last year's 0-2 ending and 10-0 CFP loss to Miami plus another loss to rival Texas was hard on fans, but HC Elko is popular even if this year is just decent / transitional. LB and team captain Sanford injured in spring will miss season. Have yet to beat Texas (0-2) since joining the SEC.  Tier 2-3 SEC team.
team_notes.vanderbilt: All-everything QB Diego Pavia graduated after leading Vanderbilt to its best two seasons maybe ever — can they win without him? Tier 3-5 SEC team.
